ucs.title: [LLM] Aoqin Dragon USB-worm propagation via fake-folder/document executable from removable media (Mongall/Evernote Tray dropper)
ucs.description: Hunts Aoqin Dragon's signature initial-access and propagation chain: a PE file masquerading as a folder or document icon (e.g. 'Evernote Tray Application', fake .doc/.xls folders) executed from a removable drive, which is the group's documented entry vector into SE-Asian government and telco networks since 2013.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process values(Processes.process_hash) as process_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Device\\Harddisk*\\DR*", "?:\\*.exe") AND (match(Processes.process_path,"^[A-Z]:\\\\[^\\\\]+\\.exe$") OR Processes.process_name IN ("Evernote Tray Application.exe","My Documents.exe","Recycler.exe","USBDisk.exe","Removable Disk.exe") OR match(Processes.process_name,"\\.(doc|docx|xls|xlsx|pdf|jpg|folder)\\s*\\.exe$"))) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_hash _time 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| join type=left host process_path [| tstats summariesonly=true count as removable_writes from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("?:\\*") AND Filesystem.action=created by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path | rename Filesystem.dest as host Filesystem.file_path as process_path] 
| where removable_writes>0 OR like(process_path,"%\\Device\\Harddisk%") 
| eval reason="Aoqin Dragon USB worm – folder/doc icon EXE on removable drive" 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` 
| table firstTime lastTime host user process_name process_path parent_process_name process_hash reason
ucs.kql:
let removableExec = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FolderPath matches regex @"^[A-Z]:\\[^\\]+\.exe$"
   or FolderPath has_any ("\\Device\\Harddisk", "\\$Recycle.Bin\\")
   or FileName in~ ("Evernote Tray Application.exe","My Documents.exe","Recycler.exe","USBDisk.exe","Removable Disk.exe","New folder.exe","Documents.exe")
   or FileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(doc|docx|xls|xlsx|pdf|jpg|folder)\s*\.exe$";
let removableWrites = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where FolderPath matches regex @"^[A-Z]:\\" and FolderPath !startswith "C:\\"
| where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".lnk"
| project DeviceId, DropPath=FolderPath, DropFile=FileName, DropTime=Timestamp, DropSHA256=SHA256;
removableExec
| join kind=leftouter (removableWrites) on DeviceId
| where isnotempty(DropPath) or FolderPath has "\\Device\\Harddisk"
| extend Reason="Aoqin Dragon: folder/doc-masquerade EXE executed from removable media"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine, DropFile, DropPath, Reason
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Aoqin Dragon's hallmark TTP since 2013 (per SentinelOne 2022) is a removable-media worm that drops a PE file using a fake folder/Evernote-tray icon — victims double-click the 'folder' and execute the dropper. Joining process-from-removable-drive with a same-host removable-drive write event filters out generic USB-launcher noise and pins the activity to this actor's specific delivery chain rather t

ucs.title: [LLM] Aoqin Dragon Heyoka backdoor DNS-tunneling C2 (Mongall second-stage beacon)
ucs.description: Hunts the Heyoka custom backdoor — Aoqin Dragon's exclusive forked DNS-exfil tool — by detecting high-entropy, long-subdomain DNS queries from a recently-spawned non-browser process, which is how Mongall stagers establish C2 against air-gap-adjacent SE-Asian government targets.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count values(DNS.query) as queries dc(DNS.query) as unique_queries avg(len(DNS.query)) as avg_qlen max(len(DNS.query)) as max_qlen from datamodel=Network_Resolution where DNS.message_type=QUERY AND DNS.record_type IN ("TXT","A","AAAA","NULL") by DNS.src DNS.dest host _time span=10m 
| where unique_queries>50 AND avg_qlen>40 AND max_qlen>60 
| eval shannon_sample=mvindex(queries,0) 
| `ut_shannon(shannon_sample)` 
| where ut_shannon>3.8 
| join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name!="chrome.exe" Processes.process_name!="msedge.exe" Processes.process_name!="firefox.exe" Processes.process_name!="svchost.exe" Processes.process_name!="nslookup.exe" by host Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process_name as proc parent_process_name as parent] 
| eval reason="Aoqin Dragon Heyoka DNS-tunneling C2 candidate" 
| table _time host DNS.src DNS.dest proc parent user unique_queries avg_qlen max_qlen ut_shannon reason
ucs.kql:
let dnsBeacons = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "DnsConnectionInspected" or RemotePort == 53
| extend qname = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).query)
| where isnotempty(qname) and strlen(qname) > 50
| extend sub = tostring(split(qname, ".")[0])
| extend entropy = log2(toreal(toscalar(
      array_length(extract_all(@"[A-Za-z0-9]", sub))))) // proxy length-uniqueness
| summarize qcount=count(), unique_q=dcount(qname), max_len=max(strlen(qname)), avg_len=avg(strlen(qname)), sample=any(qname)
  by DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, RemoteIP, bin(Timestamp, 10m)
| where qcount > 50 and avg_len > 40 and max_len > 60
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","svchost.exe","nslookup.exe","dns.exe")
| extend Reason="Aoqin Dragon Heyoka DNS tunneling – long high-cardinality subdomain queries from non-browser process"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, RemoteIP, qcount, unique_q, avg_len, max_len, sample, Reason
| sort by qcount desc
ucs.rationale: Heyoka is a publicly-documented fork of an open-source DNS-tunneling proof-of-concept that Aoqin Dragon (UNC94) uniquely weaponized for stealthy C2 in environments where SE-Asian government targets restrict outbound HTTP. Generic APT detections look for HTTP beacons — this UC keys on the actor's DNS-tunneling fingerprint (long, high-entropy subdomains, high cardinality, from non-browser binaries)
